title: Cystopteris elata
intro: Cystopteris elata, also known as the tall wood fern, is a species of fern native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cystopteris elata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cystopteris elata is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, often used to treat colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair.
taxonomy: Cystopteris elata, also known as the tall wood fern, belongs to the family Cystopteridaceae. This species is classified in the genus Cystopteris and is a type of vascular plant. At the kingdom level, Cystopteris elata is classified as Plantae, and at the phylum level, it is classified as Pteridophyta. The class and subclass levels classify it as Polypodiopsida and Polypodiidae, respectively. It is further classified as a fern, specifically an epipetric fern, which grows on rocks and trees.
